source_doc:
These beautiful things that I've got.
And then Benson, that front flip.
What were you thinking?
I was not thinking.
You had started doing flips when I was a little a little boy, and I thought he was the coolest.
So when I was like three, I would try them off my couch and it progressed and progressed to the front yard and then on stage.

source_doc:
Hello?
Hello?
Who's there?
My name is Emily.
Who are you?
I am Sam.
Who are these people?
I don't know.
They had masks on, so I didn't get to see.
Are there others?
Others?
Yeah.
Like.
Like us?
I don't know.

source_doc:
One more.
Michael.
Last bracket.
Cherry on the cupcake.
Some of them like two colors.
Should we do something wild or passable?
MM.
This hand is wild.
This hand is passable.
Which one?
Wild.
Okay, I don't want to make you look like Christmas, though.
You always close your eyes at the dentist and the orthodontist, too.
MM.
Oh.

source_doc:
I am waiting, professor.
Well, what do you see in my hand?
I see the hand of a of a charming young man.
Within the next few months Lord Arthur will go on a voyage.
Naturally, his honeymoon.
And lose a relative.
Well, we all lose relatives.
Sometimes I think they were created for the purpose.
Can you tell us any more?
No, Lady Clementine.
That's all I can say.

source_doc:
They got so many trains out there, they got a hard time keeping them running into each other.
Got trains going every which way.
Got people on all of them.
Somebody going, well, somebody just left.
I believe everybody stayed in one place.
This be a better world.
Now.
What I done learnt in 27 years of railroading is this.

That personality hasn't changed and can't change because that's the world of the show.
There's two of you.
There's Mr. Pickles, and then there's Jeff.
And never the two should meet in order to prevent the destruction of them both.

Hey there.
Thump, thump.

The last time I was out here, you could see 27 miles across northwest Alabama.
You getting tired of the view?
No.
The trouble is that the birds keep on flying into the glass because they can't see it and break their wings.
Well, why don't you just throw an old fishnet over the window?
That way you'd have your view and your birds.
Well, I just guess and calculate.
I never thought of it.
You know, I swear, if a fly was to bust his leg, he'd come running with a splint.

source_doc:
Tinkering with my stereo system this week has got me thinking about an oddity that I've never been able to get to the bottom of...let's see if the Mastodon hive mine can help.
The amp is a Philips FA454 and the CD Player is a Philips CD150.
According to the amp manual I can connect the units with a "8-pole DIN to 5-pole DIN + 2 x single-pole CINCH" cable."
I take this to mean an 8 pin din to a 5 pin + stereo RCA cable...which sounds cursed.
Never been able to find one. Can anyone make one?

The service manual for the amp is nice and clear (picture 2 in above toot), but I can't find anything as nice for the CD player. I've attached a picture (picture 4 in the above toot) from the service manual that I *think* is the relevant section.
Currently I have the CD player hooked up with just the RCA cables and it all works fine, but if I could find/make the correct Din to Din + RCA cable then I could use some additional functionality and free up the only RCA stereo inputs on my amp.

There's a repair café this weekend in the town where I live...if I'm still puzzling over it all by then I might take it all along there and see if they can help me hack together a cable.
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Dán vahku go lean duddjodan iežan stereofápmorágadusain, de álgen smiehttat ovtta ártetvuođa birra maid in leat goasge nagodan čoavdit... geahččat vel nagoda go Mastodon-máttafápmu veahkehit mu.
Várreámpá lea Philips FA454 ja CD-spealli lea Philips CD150.
Ámpá giehtakirji mield sáhtán ovttastit ovttadagaid "8-navvásaš DIN 5-navvásaš DIN + 2 x ovttanavvásaš CINCH" -kábelijn."
Mun ipmirdan dán nu ahte dat lea 8-pinnat din 5-pinnat + stereo RCA-kabelii... mii gullustallá fasttin.
In leat goasge gávdnan dakkára. Sáhttágo oktage ráhkadit dakkára?

Ámpá divvungiehtakirji lea čorgat ja čielggas (govva 2 badjelis dán toot-reivves), muhto in gávdna maidege nu čorgga CD-speallái. Lean bidjan mieldi gova (govva 4 badjelis dán toot-reivves) divvungiehtakirjjis, mii mu *mielas* lea dat áššáigullevaš oassi.
Dál mus lea CD-spealli ovttastuvvon dušše RCA-kábelijguin ja buot doaibmá bures, muhto jos gávnnášin/ráhkadivččen dan rievttes Din-Din + RCA-kábela, de sáhtášin geavahit muhtun lassidoaimmaid ja beasášin eret áidna RCA-stereočatnagiin mu ámpás.

Dán vahkkoloahpa lea divvunkafea dán gávpogis gos mun ássan... jos dasto ain smiehtan dán, de soaittán váldit buot dán fárrui dohko ja geahččat sáhttet go sii veahkehit mu gođđot dakkár kábela.

Ámpá divvungiehtakirji lea čorgat ja čielggas (govva 2 badjelis dán toot-reivves), muhto in gávdna maidege nu čorgga CD-speallái. Lean bidjan mieldi gova (govva 4 badjelis dán toot-reivves) divvungiehtakirjjis, mii mu *mielas* lea dat áššáigullevaš oassi.
Dál mus lea CD-spealli ovttastuvvon dušše RCA-kábelijguin ja buot doaibmá bures, muhto jos gávnnášin/ráhkadivččen dan rievttes Din-Din + RCA-kábela, de sáhtášin geavahit muhtun lassidoaimmaid ja beasášin eret áidna RCA-stereočatnagiin mu ámpás.

Dán vahkkoloahpa lea divvunkafea dán gávpogis gos mun ássan... jos dasto ain smiehtan dán, de soaittán váldit buot dán fárrui dohko ja geahččat sáhttet go sii veahkehit mu gođđot dakkár kábela.

And just throughout all the years, like I've never, I've never had to, to second guess you or think if you know what you were saying was was real or not.
Like you.
Um, I don't know.
You've just always been very clear intentioned and never had to worry about you.
I don't know.
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is automatically transcribed from spoken language and can contain errors. Maintain the flow and colloquial style of the speaker in the translation. Do not include non-linguistic sounds (e.g. laughter, groans, hesitation sounds, etc.), but do include interjections. If a word is interrupted, either guess the full word if possible or otherwise omit it. Keep foreign words as they are when translating. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Output the text such that each sentence is on a separate line. Nugo don.
Hmm, in dieđe.

source_doc:
@Edent ah, interesting - was the Hub 5 something you had to request from Virgin, or did you just get one elsewhere and set it up? (As per recent discussion was issued with a 5x, with its lack Modem Mode.)

@yvan I already had the Hub 4. I went onto their online chat and whinged about poor download speeds until they sent me a new Hub.
If you're on the XPON network, I don't think you have a choice though.

@Edent ah yeah, just been trying to do some research, but it's all a bit muddy... I'm FTTP/XGS-PON so I think just stuck with the 5x... not an insurmountable problem, just a little annoying. (But compared to unreliable <40mpbs BT rotting fenland copper that cost me more than Virgin... I'm not really complaining.)
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
@Edent ah, gelddolaš - fertiideatgo don bivdit Hub 5 Virginis, vai oaččutgo dan eará sajis ja heivehit dan? (Nu mo gieskat lei sáhka, de mun ožžon 5x, mas váldá Modem Mode.)

@yvan mus lei juo Hub 4. Mun čálle sin neahttaságastallamii ja váidaledje heajos viežžanspiiddaid birra dassážiigo sii sáddeje munnje ođđa Hub.
Jos leat XPON-fierpmádagas, de in jáhke ahte dus lea dattetge eará molssaeaktu.

@Edent ah joo, lean juste geahččalan dutkat ášši, muhto buot lea veahá eahpesielggas... mus lea FTTP/XGS-PON, nu ahte jáhkán ahte lean fástá dan 5x-iin... ii dat leat muttomaš čoavdetmeahttun váttisvuohta, lea dušše veahá fasti. (Muhto jos buohtastahttá eahpesihkaris <40mpbs BT rottejeaddji jeagge-veaikkiin mii maxii munnje eanet go Virgin... de in mun rievtti mielde váidda.)

source_doc:
Where is he, Bill?
Out there.
Don't you girls back down tonight?
We got a surprise for you.
That sounds exciting.
Tommy, just you wait and see.
Hello?
Hello.
Hello, Jim.
They told me it was somebody about the hall for tonight.
You don't think they'd let me in the kitchen if they knew I was organizing the strike, do you?
You're pretty smart, aren't you?
Can you get away?
Yes.

source_doc:
Traction meets fashion: The best weatherproof trainers for rain-slicked pavements

The Cloudhorizon Waterproof feels purpose-built for the stop-start rhythm of winter commuting. Its lightweight waterproof upper keeps your feet dry without adding bulk, and the shoe's smooth rocker shape helps you roll forward easily, even when you're layered up and carrying heavy loads.

Underfoot, Helion foam creates a cushioned bounce that softens long walks between stations, while the grippy rubber outsole keeps things steady on wet steps and shiny tiles. The pared-back design gives it a polished, modern look, ideal if you prefer a trainer that handles lousy weather subtly, without shouting its technical credentials.

hypo_0:
Luovvan jorri deaivá máde: Buoremus dálkesihkaris hárjehallangápmagat njuoska ja slirras bálgáide

Cloudhorizon Waterproof orru leamen duddjojuvvon juste dálvvejohtolaga gaskkalduvvi rithmii. Dan geahpes ja čáhcedavvi bajilassi doallá du juolggi goihkan almmá badjelmearálaš lossadaga haga, ja gápmaga njuovžilis hápmi veahkeha du ovdánit álkit, maiddái dalle go leat gárvodan gassa biktasiiguin ja guoddát lossa nođiid.

Juolgevuložis Helion-skupma duddjo litna ja njuovžilis buncama, mii lžžeha guhkes vázzimiid gaskal stašuvnnaid, ja gáržžes gume-olggoš doallá dilit dássidin njuoska ráinnas ja šlattuhahtti láhtteruvttuin. Smihtis ja ovttageardán hápmi addá dasa čorgis ja ođđaáigásaš olgguldas hámi, mii lea eanas heivvolaš jus hálidat hárjehallangápmaga mii duste dálkkiid suddubasat, almmá iežas teknihkalaš iešvuođaid čalmmustahttima haga.

Dan geahpes, čáhcetastti bajilassi doallá du juolggi goihkan almmá gápmaga lossudahttima haga, ja gápmaga jorba hápmi veahkeha du rullat ovddasguvlui álkit, maiddái dalle go leat gárvodan gassa biktasiiguin ja guoddát lossa nođiid.

Juolgevuložis Helion-skupma duddjo litna buncama, mii láhžeha guhkes vázzimiid gaskal stašuvnnaid, ja nannosit gáržžejeaddji gume-olggoš doallá dilit dássidin njuoska ráinnas ja šlahttojeaddji láhtteruvttuin. Smihtis hápmi addá dasa čorgis ja ođđaáigásaš olgguldas hámi, mii lea eanas heivvolaš jus hálidat hárjehallangápmaga mii duste heajos dálkkiid suddubasat, almmá iežas teknihkalaš iešvuođaid čalmmustahttima haga.

source_doc:
Whoa, you mean Blender Market, the thing I said was the first step towards the Blender environment becoming a shitty subscription service, renamed itself to the oh-so-appealing name "Superhive" and then decided to mandate subscriptions?
WHOA WHO COULD HAVE GUESSED?! Not me, that's for sure. This is completely out of left field!

TBH, I have never seen a single company with "Hive" in the name that wasn't a complete shitshow run by assholes, so I never bought anything on that platform -
Sorry
I didn't RENT anything on that platform if I could help it.
Maybe it's unfair, but "clearly we, a company that specializes in connecting creators with their audience, should name ourselves HIVE, because the creators are just our loyal little bees, and audience is our loyal little bees, work for us!" always gives me bad vibes.

"Hive implies busy crowds!"
Hive implies a queen.
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Vuoi, oaivvildat go Blender Market, fal dat mii mu mielas lei vuosttaš lávki dán guvlui ahte Blender-biras šaddá veahjallas diŋgonbálvalussan, lea rievdadan namas dakkár gelddolaš namman go "Superhive" ja dasto mearridan gáibidit diŋgomiid?
VUOI GIITU SÁHTII DÁN ARVVEDALLAT?! In goit mun, dat lea sihkkar. Dát bođii dego gáicá guolbanis!

Duođaid daddjodettiin, in leat goassige oaidnán ovttage fitnodaga mas lea "Hive" namas, mii ii livčče leamaš áivan gurgulas ja maid fasttes olbmot jođihit, nu ahte in leat goassige oastán maidege dán vuođđovuogádagas -
Ándagassii
In LÁIKON maidege dán vuođđovuogádagas, jus fal sáhtiin dan vealtat.
Eahpegába, muhto "mii, fitnodat mii lea earenoamážit čeahppi duddjot oktavuođaid duddjondahkkiid ja olbmuid gaskkas, berret rávkat iežamet namain HIVE, d損e go duddjondahkkiat leat dušše min oskkáldas smávva uvssot, ja olbmot leat min oskkáldas smávva uvssot, bargat min ovddas!" addá munnje álo fasttes dovdduid.

"Hive čájeha ahte doppe lea gáržžes ja eallálas olmmošmuttot!"
Hive čájeha ahte doppe lea dronnet.

In LÁIKON maidege dán vuođđovuogádagas, jus fal sáhtiin dan vealtat.
Eahpegába, muhto "mii, fitnodat mii lea earenoamážit čeahppi duddjot oktavuođaid duddjondahkkiid ja gehččiid gaskkas, berret rávkat iežamet namain HIVE, d損e go duddjondahkkiat leat dušše min oskkáldas smávva uvssot, ja gehččit leat min oskkáldas smávva uvssot, bargat min ovddas!" addá munnje álo fasttes dovdduid.

"Hive čájeha ahte doppe lea gáržžes ja eallálas olmmošmuttot!"
Hive čájeha ahte doppe lea dronnet.

source_doc:
@Maker_of_Things
Is there any bruising or swelling? If it’s swelling then let the practice know and keep an eye on it.
Assuming she took it from the veins around the elbow, theres a chance she went too deep and hit the back wall of the vein, and an underlying tendon then bruised it on the way out. All depends on technique and equipment. I could never get the hang of a fixed needle draw but was a deft hand at a butterfly draw, you could be much kinder.
Hope it’s eased now.

@tempusfelix
Yeah, from my elbow. It is still sore, but getting better. No bruising or bleeding though.

@Maker_of_Things
Good. She may well have been too deep so remember if they’re not urgent, you can often ask for a specific practitioner if your surgery allows it. For example I like the sister at mine, she’s so smooth.

Leago doppe mangelgádjan dahje bávččastupmi? Jos lea bávččastupmi, de almmit dan doaktárbáikái ja čuovvul dili.
Navddán ahte son válddii vara giehtagávviloras, ja lea dakkár vejolašvuohta ahte son manai menddo čiekŋalit ja deaivvadii varasuona maŋitseainni, ja suona vuolde leahkki muorra de bávččagahtti dan dasto go geassgattai olggos. Buot lea duddnomuhtus ja reaidduin gitta. Mun in goasge oahppan bures geavahit gitta leahkki nállováldima, muoda ledjen hui čeahppi nállováldimis mii lea dego ganjaldat, dainna sáhtii leat eanet láđis.
Sávan ahte lea álkánedduid dál.

@tempusfelix
Juo, mu giehtagávviloras. Lea ain bávčas, muhto lea buorreneamen. Ii leat makkárge mangelgádjan dahje varra mii golgá dattetge.

@Maker_of_Things
Buorre. Son lea várra leamaš menddo čiekŋalis, nu ahte muitte, jos ii leat hoahppu, sáhtát dávjá bivdit dihto bargi jos du doaktárgárdi dan diktá. Ovdamearkka dihte mun liikon min doaktárgárddi buohccedikšui, son lea nu njuovžil.

Na buorre, mun áiggun dás ráhkadit pasta.
Dakkáriin, mii, mun in dieđe doaibmágo dat.
Nu dán galgá bargat.
Vuorde, maid don bargat?
Mun bargan dán.
Munnje ii liiko.
Dat.
Ná dán galgá bargat.
Mun lean geahččan.
Gánddat.
Dát lea.
Ii mihkkege mana boastut.
Don válddát jáffočoakka, veháš sáltti, seaguhat dan, ráhkadat unna roggeža gasku, ja dohko don luitestat veháš oliveoljju.
Muhto eanaš dakkár mani sturrosaš meari aquafaba-golggiidusa.

source_doc:
Progress after a while off. I got the sewing machine out to do the bill and a crown seam. Half the crown sewn plus the bill, resting atop the paper form made from the pattern. I had to rip and re-sew the machine stitch because I pinned along instead of across the seam and went off track. I might re-do the bill if I want a little more depth.
#millinery

Eight crown panels and two bill panels assembled into three pieces and pinned for general shape. Sachi's Studio Newsboy Cap pattern in green velvet. Mostly machine stitched.
The pattern calls for topstitching along the seams of the crown panels, which might suggest either matching or contrasting thread.
#millinery

Top view before I sew the crown halves together. Reviewing the how-to video. I see Sachi is using a few tiny spring clips during sewing instead of pinning the whole seam.

A bit of a messy join on the eight panels coming together at the peak of the newsboy cap, so I am thinking a larger button may be in order to disguise the seams.

Crown fully pieced, with cloth covered.button at peak. Raw edges loosely tucked under. Seams neither pressed nor topstitched.
#millinery
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Ođđa ovdáneapmi veaháš botta maŋŋel. Viežžan goarrunmássinna vai beasan goarrut gáffala ja čoarvegoarvvi. Bealli čoarvvis ja gáffal leat gorrojuvvon, ja dat rusat báhpirhápmi badjel mii lea ráhkaduvvon minstara mielde. Šaddegohten gurrehit ja goarrut mássinnagárvvi ođđasit, go giddejin neđđimiid ravdda mielde iige doarrás, ja de gárrtan luhčadit. Sáhtán goarrut gáffala ođđasit, juos hálidan veahá eanet čiekŋodaga.
#millinery

Gávcci čoarvebihtá ja guokte gáffalbihtá leat biddjon golbman oassin ja giddejuvvon neđđimiiguin vai hápmi oidno. Sachi’s Studio Newsboy Cap -minstar ruoná silkis. Eanas gorrojuvvon mássinnain.
Minstara mielde galgá goarrut baddoravdda čoarvebihtáid sámaid mielde, mii mearkkaša ahte sáhttá geavahit juogu ovttalágán dahje earálágán suttu.
#millinery

Oidno badjegeahčen ovdal go goarun čoarvebeliid oktii. Geahčan rávvebinná. Oidno ahte Sachi geavaha moadde unna gizzunbinnáža goarruma áiggis neđđemiid sajis.

Veahá eahpečielgas čoahkkáigeassi gávcci bihtás mat deivvadit gahpira baddomuttus, nu ahte smiehtan ahte stuorrát boallu livččii buorre vai sámeat gokčat goarrunjatnasiid.

Čoarvi lea ollásit gárvvis, ja gokčojuvvon boallu lea baddomuttus. Roaves ravddat leat ložžasit biddjon vuollái. Sámat eai leat deaddiluvvon eai ge baddogorrojuvvon.
#millinery
hypo_1:

source_doc:
I'm doing a eekly 1hr meeting where I'm going over with a director of engineering my soap box on managing a product management organization and actually it's coming out to be a pretty good episodic thing

Topics have been
- different types of product managers and the basic skill sets in developing requirements
- how product managers ensure an ongoing cadence for an engineering team
- dealing with product managers with varying backgrounds (engineering, data analyst, operational SME, consulting etc)
what's been fun is that this Director and I worked at a previous company with product managers that I didn't manage and I'm able to reference "hey remember that person? that person was awesome and here's why."

so in various email chains and slack threads I'll forward them to said director (if he's already not in there) and be like "ok a GOOD product manager would do THIS" and a "BAD product maanger would do THAT" rememember when so and so did THIS? that was good.

hypo_0:
Mus lea vahkkosaš 1-tiimma čoahkkin muhtun inšenevravuogádagaid direktovrain, gos mun muitalan iežan oaiviliid das movt jođihit buvttahálddašeaddjiid organisašuvnna, ja duođai dás lea šaddamin hui buorre ráidu

Fáttát leat leamaš

source_doc:
Local stable out to cash in at Coffs with four contenders

Local trainer Noel Mayfield-Smith is hoping to have a lucrative day at Sunday's Coffs Harbour meeting with four runners stepping out for the stable on the six-race card.

The Group 1-winning horseman will start his day in the second event of the day when Ausbred Yahoo contests the Komatsu Country Boosted Benchmark 58 Handicap (1605m) with two-kilogram claiming apprentice Ella Drew taking the ride.

Racing returns to Coffs Harbour on Sunday.

Ausbred Yahoo has been racing in good form with a third placing at Kempsey three starts ago being followed by two successive seconds at Coffs Harbour, the latest when beaten by Sunday's rival Caledonian.

Mayfield-Smith links with another apprentice, three-kilogram claimer Leanne Boyd, when Never Island starts in the Insurance Advisernet Australia Pink Silks F&M Benchmark 58 Handicap (1305m).

A winner of three of her 15 starts, Never Island will carry 59.5 kilograms after Boyd's allowance. Never Island was beaten less than a length when second behind Montgomery at Grafton and backed that up with a last-start fifth at Coffs Harbour behind In The Fine Print when again beaten less than a length.

Raymond Spokes takes the ride on the Mayfield-Smith-prepared Monte Cruise in the O'Brien Plumbing Relining Wayne Glenn Pink Silks Cup Benchmark 82 Handicap (1205m).

Monte Cruise is also a three-time winner from just the nine starts. She hasn't raced since July when finishing last of eight at Eagle Farm and has had two trials at this track in preparation for her return, finishing second behind Saturday's Wyong winner Petticoat before winning the latest of them.

Mayfield-Smith rounds out the day when he again links with Boyd when she rides Cardsharp in the Key Community Group C,G&E Benchmark 58 Handicap (1305m).
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is a news article. Ensure the translation is formal and consistent with journalistic standards. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Báikkálaš stábila áigu rásset Coffsas njeallje gilvaleaddjiin

Báikkálaš hárjeheaddji Noel Mayfield-Smith doaivvo gánnehahtti beaivvi sotnabeivvi Coffs Harbour -gilvvuin, gos stábilas leat njeallje doloža guđa rásseašmuttus.

Group 1 -vuoiti heastadovdi álggaha beaivvis beaivvi nuppi dáhpáhusas, go Ausbred Yahoo searvá Komatsu Country Boosted Benchmark 58 Handicap (1605m) -gilvui, mas guokte kilo unnideaddji oahpahalli Ella Drew vuodjá.

Heastagilvvut máhccet Coffs Harbouras sotnabeivvi.

Ausbred Yahoo lea leamaš buorres hámis gilvvuin; golbma gilvvu áigi Kempseyas son šaddas goalmmádin, ja dan maŋŋá leat boahtán guokte maŋitgohtet nubbiriid Coffs Harbouras. Maŋimusas son vuittihaladuvvui sotnabeivvi vuostálasti Caledonianii.

Mayfield-Smith ovttastuvvá nuppiin oahpahallijn, golbma kilo unnideaddji Leanne Boydiin, go Never Island searvá Insurance Advisernet Australia Pink Silks F&M Benchmark 58 Handicap (1305m) -gilvui.

Never Island lea vuoitán golbma 15 gilvustis, ja son guoddá 59,5 kilo maŋŋá go Boyda allowance lea váldojuvvon eret. Never Island vuittihaladuvvui unnit go ovtta jálkka-gaskka go son lei nubbi Montgomery maŋis Graftonas, ja son nannii dán maŋimus gilvvu viđat sajiin Coffs Harbouras In The Fine Print maŋis, go vuittihaladuvvui fas unnit go ovtta jálkka-gaskka.

Raymond Spokes vuodjá Mayfield-Smitha hárjehan Monte Cruise heasttain O'Brien Plumbing Relining Wayne Glenn Pink Silks Cup Benchmark 82 Handicap (1205m) -gilvustis.

Monte Cruise lea maid golmma gearddi vuoiti dušše ovcci gilvvu gaskkas. Son ii leat gilvohallan suoidnemánu maŋŋá, go son šaddas maŋimussan gávcci gaskkas Eagle Farmas, ja son lea hárjehallan guokte gearddi dán luoddas máhccamii ráhkkanan dihte. Son šaddas nubbin lávvardaga Wyonga vuoiti Petticoata maŋis ovdal go vuittii dán maŋimus hárjehallama.

Mayfield-Smith loahpaha beaivvi go fas ovttastuvvá Boydiin, go son vuodjá Cardsharpa Key Community Group C,G&E Benchmark 58 Handicap (1305m) -gilvustis.

Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is automatically transcribed from spoken language and can contain errors. Maintain the flow and colloquial style of the speaker in the translation. Čiegus liegga gáldut.
Man somá.
Mii leat dál boahtán dáidda čiegus liegga gálduide.

Don manaat 0,06 dollara badjel.
Mun dárbbahan dan 0,06 dollara.
Muhto mun háliidan dušše juste 10 dollara.
Don pumpejit 10,06 ovddas.
Dalle gálggat divvut bumaideat nu ahte dat eai mana nu jođánit.
Mus lea fástá sisaboahtu, dieđusge.
Áhkku, jos don it.

source_doc:
and it's not a bad film — it's a spectacular-looking one in spots — but it is also a film entirely made out of other, better and more thematically cohesive films. so it's 2001 and the three-body problem and arrival and wall-e a little bit, but it's also Quips Sexyman Saves The World Part XVII and ultimately just not that lot

while watching i wondered why the earth needed to be in peril, and if the other two members of the multicultural crew dying to leave the White Man in charge was some kind of an anti-woke statement; but in light of some of the book author andy weir's 'most media literate libertarian' comments about how star trek should be just about spaceships going pew-pew, at least to him the answer is yes to the latter and 'fascism' to the former

@esvrld I read the book a little while back and I may have given him too much credit, especially given his recent decision to open his mouth, but I took the death of the international crew to strand the white boy as an understanding that his second book failed because he is not any good at writing people interacting with each other and his only skill is creating clever little puzzles for his self-insert to solve in complete isolation. Giving him an alien buddy was a good move because it opens the possibility of character interaction without requiring it to resemble the way humans relate to one another

Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
iige dat leat heajos filbma — muhtun osiin dat lea hui eallu ja čáppa — muhto dat lea maiddái filbma mii lea ollásit ráhkaduvvon eará, buoret ja fáttá dáfus eambbo oktiivásttolaš filmmain. nu ahte dat lea 2001 ja golmma goruda čuolbma ja arrival ja veaháš wall-e, muhto dat lea maiddái Quips Sexyman Saves The World Part XVII ja loahpa loahpas ii fal dat mii das galgai leat

gčadettiin mun smiehtasin ahte manne eana ferte leat áitojuvvon, ja ahte leago dat mii guovtti eará máŋggakultuvrralaš bárašeami lahtas jábmet ames Vilgis Almmái beassat stivret juogalágan anti-woke-cealkámuš; muhto girječálli andy weir muhtun 'eanas mediafáhtalaš libertariana' cealkámušaid vuođul das makkár star trek galggašii leat dušše gievranasgirdimat mat bálistit pew-pew, de unnimustá sutnje vástádus lea ahte dán maŋit áššái lea jua ja ovddit áššái 'fasisma'

@esvrld Mun logen girjji muhtun áigi dás ovdal ja soitet ledjen sutnje menddo buorre, erenoamážit go son dál gieskat mearridii hupmat, muhto mun ipmirdin dan riikkaidgaskasaš bárašeami jápmima mii guđii vilgis bártni okto nu, ahte son iešge ipmirda ahte su nubbi girji feilii dan dáfus go son ii máhte čállit olbmooktiivuođaid birra ja su áidna dáidu lea ráhkadit jierbmes unna bázalaččaid maid su iežas-aláhusaš karaktera galgá čoavdit ollislaš oktonasvuođas. Dat ahte son oaččui olgoriikkalaš ustit lea buorre mearrádus, go dat addá vejolašvuođa karakteriid ovttastallamii almmá gáibit ahte dat sulastahtášii dan mot olbmot gulahallet guhtet guimmiineaset

source_doc:
(1/?)
@lorry
> Bumble is dire at Trust and Safety
Care to expand on that or link me?
> Whitney (Bumble) [is] a billionaire anyway, so what does she care?
I presume she didn't found Bumble by herself, maybe wasn't even part of the original group (as with Melon Husk and Tesla). What about the rest of them?
@screwlisp

(2/?)
@lorry
> I imagine there's still space for going back to the old model
There's plenty of new models to be tried that would be a huge improvement over playing hashtag roulette, either on DataFarms or decentralised social networks. One idea I saw was online dating as a group-based social experience. This is a handwave in the right direction. As is @cy's idea about matchmakers in another branch of this thread.

(3/?)
Speaking of roulette, what about a Chat Roulette style speed-dating app?
The app could;
* perform some basic T&S vetting (to minimise unsolicited dick-cam, etc), including confirming what general area you're in (ask me how!)
* throw you into a round of realtime speed dating with others in your area (text? Voice? Video? see 4/4).
* As with normal speed dating, if you both Favourite each other after talking, you get each others' contacts
#OnlineDating #ChatRoulette

@strypey I nearly joined #Chatroulette, but then Covid and the Ukraine war started, so that never went anywhere. I had a different direction I wanted to take them, though.
#Bumble #TrustAndSafety ? Well, the industry isn't exactly full of dating site whistleblowers, but safety workers in the industry do talk to me. I know more than I would ever want to know about the inside workings of most dating sites, and sadly. It's not really an amazing revelation that Bumble is as bad as the rest, though.
None of the dating sites puts more than a veneer of effort into T&S. They weigh the cost of litigation against the cost of actually doing the work, and so far, litigation costs have always been lower.

@lorry
> he industry isn't exactly full of dating site whistleblowers
Any particular reason for that?
> They weigh the cost of litigation against the cost of actually doing the work, and so far, litigation costs have always been lower
Huh. The Fight Club principle at work. Sad. We deserve better from our digital matchmaking tools.

Hálidat go čilget eambbo dehe sáddet munnje liŋkka?
> Whitney (Bumble) lea juogalágan billonára goitge, nu ahte manne son beroštivččii?
Jáhkán ahte son ii vuođđudan Bumble okto, dehe ii soaitán leat mieldege álgojoavkkus (nugo Melon Husk ja Tesla oktavuođas). Na mii dasto rabažiiguin?
@screwlisp

(2/?)
@lorry
> Jáhkán ahte lea ain sadji máhccat boares modellii
Leat olu ođđa mállet maid sáhttá geahččalit, mat livčče ollu buoret go emojidiehto-ruletta, juogo DataFarms-bálvalusain dehe desentraliserejuvvon sosiála fierpmeádjáin. Okta jurdda maid oidnen, lei neahttadeatnan joavkoveahkásaš sosiála fearánin. Dát lea buorre hástalus rivttes guvlui. Nugo maid @cy jurdda gaskkusteaddjiid birra nuppi oasis dán sága.

(3/?)
Go rulettas háladit, de mii livččii Chat Roulette -stilla speed-dating-applikašuvdna?
Applikašuvdna sáhtášii;
* dahkat muhtun vuođđo dorvolašvuođatabeanttaid (unnidan dihte eahpeháliiduvvon dárki-kameraid jna.), mieldedistiin maid nannema makkár guovllus don leat (jeara mus mo!)
* bálkestit du njuolggosáttemirkko speed-dating-ruandii earáiguin du guovllus (teaksta? Jietna? Video? geahča 4/4).
* Nugo dábálaš speed-datingas, jos gurrebehtet nuppiideat liikojeaddjin maŋŋel go lehpet háleštan, de oažžubehtet nuppiideat oktavuođadieđuid
#OnlineDating #ChatRoulette

@strypey Ledjen lahka searvat #Chatroulette-bálvalussii, muhto de álggii Covid ja Ukraina soahti, nu ahte dat ii gullan gosage. Mus lei muđui eará guovlu maid hálidin dainna váldit.
#Bumble #TrustAndSafety ? No, dát suorgi ii dárkkis leat dievva deatnanbáikkiid whistlebloweriin, muhto dorvvolašvuođabargit dán suorggis háledit munnje. Dieđán eambbo go hálidivččen diehtit eanáš deatnanbáikkiid siskkáldas doaimmaid birra, ja šállošahtti gávnnahus. Ii dat dárkkis leat makkárge ámadis ođas ahte Bumble lea seamma heitto go earátge.
Ii oktage deatnanbáikkiin binnáge eambbo go bajildusat ane návcca T&S-bargui. Sii mihtidit riektelágádusa goluid dán barggu duohta bargama goluid ektui, ja dássážii riektelágádusa golut leat álo lean unnit.

@lorry
> suorgi ii dárkkis leat dievva deatnanbáikkiid whistlebloweriin
Makkárge erliid sivva dasa?
> Sii mihtidit riektelágádusa goluid dán barggu duohta bargama goluid ektui, ja dássážii riektelágádusa golut leat álo lean unnit
Aha. Fight Club -vuolggasádji barggus. Šállošahtti. Mii ánssášit buoret iežamet digitála gaskkustanneavvuin.

Leat olu ođđa mállet maid sáhttá geahččalit, mat livčče ollu buoret go hashtag-ruletta stuhkka, juogo DataFarms-bálvalusain dehe desentraliserejuvvon sosiála fierpmeádjáin. Okta jurdda maid oidnen, lei neahttadeatnan joavkoveahkásaš sosiála fearánin. Dát lea juo buorre guvlui. Nugo maid @cy jurdda gaskkusteaddjiid birra dán sága nuppi oasis.

(3/?)
Go rulettas háladit, de mii livččii Chat Roulette -stilla speed-dating-applikašuvdna?
Applikašuvdna sáhtášii;
* dahkat muhtun vuođđo T&S-dárkkistusaid (unnidan dihte eahpeháliiduvvon dárki-kameraid jna.), ja nannet makkár guovllus don leat (jeara mus mo!)
* bálkestit du njuolggosáttemirkko speed-dating-ruandii earáiguin du guovllus (teaksta? Jietna? Video? geahča 4/4).
* Nugo dábálaš speed-datingas, jos gurrebehtet nuppiideat liikojeaddjin maŋŋel go lehpet háleštan, de oažžubehtet nuppiideat oktavuođadieđuid
#OnlineDating #ChatRoulette

@strypey Ledjen lahka searvat #Chatroulette-bálvalussii, muhto de álggii Covid ja Ukraina soahti, nu ahte dat ii šaddan makkárge. Mus lei eará guovlu maid hálidin dainna váldit.
#Bumble #TrustAndSafety ? No, dát suorgi ii leat dárkkis dievva deatnanbáikkiid bálgáidgáhtejeaddjiin, muhto dorvvolašvuođabargit dán suorggis háledit munnje. Dieđán eambbo go hálidivččen diehtit eanáš deatnanbáikkiid siskkáldas doaimmaid birra, šállošahtti goit. Ii leat makkárge ámadis ođas ahte Bumble lea seamma heitto go earátge.
Ii oktage deatnanbáikkiin binnáge eambbo go bajildusat ane návcca T&S-bargui. Sii mihtidit riektelágádusa goluid dán barggu duohta bargama goluid ektui, ja dássážii riektelágádusa golut leat álo lean unnit.

> suorgi ii leat dárkkis dievva deatnanbáikkiid bálgáidgáhtejeaddjiin
Makkárge erliid sivva dasa?

Aha. Fight Club -vuolggasádji barggus. Šállošahtti. Mii ánssášit buoret iežamet digitála gaskkustanneavvuin.

#valleylink claim all #rightsofway fully subscribed for (local) power so must go outside #Row 100%

#valleylink 765kv #transmission lines carry power from ohio wv to swv to central va to #nova. look at that on a map. long way round. #rural counties seemed easier than #nova

#valleylink thunk wrong.

hypo_1:
Voi, somá.
Dá boahtá Jade.
Sáhttitgo čohkánit eará sadjái?
Eat.
Buorre dasto.
Mii čohkánat nieiddain guhte ii máhte doallat njálmmis gitta ja gii dagahii ahte don gessojit eret barggus.
Beck, mun vānum, jos livččii juoga maid sáhtášin dahkat buhtadussan dutnje, de dahan dan.
Jáhkán gal dan.

source_doc:
In a surprise Inspector Clouseau twist, I now have an extra thing to investigate for Spooky Kitties Sciences!
I was doing a quick scroll through the evenings secondary camera video, when I spotted a curious The Inspector standing on the trunk of the tree near the feeding station. Kept jumping around, finding him near where the magpies had been, so I initially suspect(edit) he was simply chasing them as they sometimes let him do, until I randomly chance stop...
#SpookyKittiesSciences

There Inspector Clouseau was, rolling around like crazing about 2 meters NE of the tree, mostly hidden by the unfortunate placement of the awnings east post. Still, there he was for 5 minutes, at which point he ran back up the tree, walked across the lawn to the hill, flattened himself at the bottom, and then ran off camera, presumably in the middle of The Zoomies.
2/
#SpookyKittiesSciences

Now, as some of my Spooky Kitties Sciences readers may know, the motivations behind that tests are not just to entertain the kitties. While the most important aspect is the genuine curiosity I had seeing the kitties rolling around in the snow, I really wanted to find something that The Inspector liked. I came out of the initial testing with only a single moment to hint that he liked something. What that was is still a mystery.
3/
#SpookyKittiesSciences
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Álggos dego muhtun fasttes Inspector Clouseau -árvalus, mus lea dál vel okta ášši maid galggan dutkat Spooky Kitties Sciences várás!
Ledjen jođánit geahčamin eahkeda nuppi bálvalankamera videoid, go fuomášin ahte dat gelddolaš The Inspector čuoččui muorramuoras biebmanbáikki lahka. Son njuikkodii doppe, ja gávdnen su lahka dan báikki gos rásttat ledje leamaš, nu ahte álggos jáhkken ahte son dušše doarridii daid dego muhtumin oažžu dahkat, dassážiigo muhtun dábálaš bisseheamis...
#SpookyKittiesSciences

Doppe Inspector Clouseau lei, jorralii dego jorran mánnu sullii 2 mehtera nuortadavás muoras, eanas čihkojuvvon dan suovaheami lulábeale čuolda boastto sajádaga dihte. Goittotge son lei doppe 5 minuhta, ja de son viegai fas muorrii, vázzilii šilju badjel várii, gopmoi eatnamii vulobealde, ja de viegai eret kameras, jáhkkimis gasku stuorimus drástema.
2/
#SpookyKittiesSciences

Dál, dego muhtun mu Spooky Kitties Sciences -lohkkit soitet diehtit, de dáid geahččalemiid duogábealde ii leat dušše dat ahte divttihit bussáid stoahkat. Vaikko deháleamos ášši lea dat eakti utnolašvuohta mii mus lei go oidnen bussáid jorralit muohttagis, de hálidin duođai gávdnat juoidá mas The Inspector liágošii. Álgo-geahččaleamis bázii dušše okta áidna boddu mii čájehii ahte son liágošii moidárii. Mii dat lei, lea ahteje velge čiegusvuohta.
3/
#SpookyKittiesSciences

source_doc:
@kagan Just use anything, even the cashier receipts from your grocery shopping - whatever you want. A friend of mine who exhibits a lot put a sheet of toilet roll from every place he's exhibiting in his scrapbook another friend got injured while hanging her frames and eventually used the cast from the injury and some gouache to stamp patters on the pages. Hence: everything's involved you want involved.

@aurora And then you... take all these things, and glue, staple, or otherwise attach them into a book? Is that it?
I'm unclear on what happens next, or why.
Honestly, I may just not be a scrapbooking person.

@kagan Yes, that's exactly the way to go! Intentionally spill your coffee, drop an ink cartridge... whatever suits you. One just needs to get all these fancy ideas shown on pinterest or whatever out of one's head. It's about documenting the process instead of being a visual dainty. Use it like a wrecking journal if that suits. It's totally up to the person who makes it.

hypo_0:
@kagan Geavat juste maid fal, vaikko gávpekuhttaid – maid gávnnat buoremussan. Mu ustit guhte doallá ollu čájáhusaid, bijai juohke čájáhusbáikkis feara makkár hivssetbábirbihtáid iežas scrap-girjái. Nubbi ustit fas gárttai rasiidda go fárpmaid heŋgii, ja loahpas son geavahgattai gipsa ja guassatempere govaid deaddileapmái siidduide. Nappo: buot mii fitna du mielas, heive searvvis.

@aurora Ja de... válddát buot dáid áššiid ja liemet, steappalat dahje earáláhkai giddet dáid girjái? Nu go?
Munnje lea veahá eahpámielas mii dasto dáhpáhuvvá, dahje manne.
Ruohtas eret, gárttán fal leat dakkár olmmoš mii ii ane ávkkis dakkár girjjiiguin.

@kagan Juo, aiddo nu dat galgá geahččaluvvot! Spille dárpahuhtto gáfe, golgahit čálahantulvvi... mii fal dutnje heive. Galgá dušše gáidat eret buot daid fiidna jurdagiid maid oaidná Pinterestis dahje eará sajis. Áššis lea sáhka proseassa dokumenteremis, iige dakkár fiidnalágan čáppahis. Geavat dán dego makkár nu "billistan-beaivegirjin" (wrecking journal) juos dat heive dutnje. Dat lea ollásit gitta olbmos iežásis.
hypo_1:
@kagan Geavat juste maid fal, vaikko borramušgávppi kuhttaid – maid ieš háliidat. Okta mu ustit gii doallá ollu čájáhusaid, bijai juohke čájáhusbáikkis hivssetbábirbihtá iežas muittogirjái. Nubbi ustit fas bávččagáhttoi go heŋgii rāmmaid, ja loahpas geavahii dan gipsa ja veahá guassaša deaddilan dihte minstariid siidduide. Nappo: buot lea mielde maid ieš háliidat fárrui.

@aurora Ja de don... válddát buot dáid áššiid, ja liemet, steappalat dahje earáláhkai giddet daid girjái? Nu go?

source_doc:
I'm so glad you're here.
Nothing better than sharing Christmas with friends and family.
I thought we could do the decorating together.
Is this the Earl of Shannon?
Indeed.
He's in the study.
Shall we?
Darling?
It's Isabella Caldwell and her lovely granddaughter.
Oh, won't she make someone a wonderful wife?
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is automatically transcribed from spoken language and can contain errors. Maintain the flow and colloquial style of the speaker in the translation. Do not include non-linguistic sounds (e.g. laughter, groans, hesitation sounds, etc.), but do include interjections. If a word is interrupted, either guess the full word if possible or otherwise omit it. Keep foreign words as they are when translating. hypo_0:
Mun lean nu ilus go don leat dás.
Ii leat mihkkege buoret go juovllaid ávvudeapmi ustibiiguin ja bearrašiin.
Mun jurddašin ahte mii sáhttit herget ovttas.
Leago dát Earl of Shannon?
Anál.
Son lea bargolanjas.
Sáhttitgo mii?
Ráhkisvuohta?
Dát lea Isabella Caldwell ja su čáppa áhkubiddu.
Oh, eai go sus šatta geasage fiidna eamit?

source_doc:
With a super comfy night in this extremely large bed, it's a good change to our small tent.
Look at this, guys.
It's got posts and everything.
Yeah, this hotel is a very old hotel and it's got loads of character to it.
It's got like loads of vintage pieces of furniture.
Look at this desk.
You just want to kind of sit there and get your fountain pen out and start.
I don't know, writing letters or something.
Established in 1860.

Geahččit dán, olbmot.
Dás leat juobe stulpput ja buot.
Juo, dát hotealla lea hui boaris hotealla ja dás lea hui ollu iešvuohta.
Dás leat dego máŋggat vintage-stiilla gálvvat.
Geahča dán čállinbeavdde.
Dás háliida dušše čohkánit, váldit gullačálloša ja álgit.
In dieđe, čállit reivviid dahje juoga dakkáraš.
Vuođđuduvvon jagi 1860.

In dieđe, čállit reivviid dahje juoga dakkáraš.
Vuođđuduvvon jagi 1860.

source_doc:
project move out, spring break relay
day 1:
Cleared main bedroom closet
& contents of boxes on one wall.
I'm surveying all my belongings. Pulling out a small percentage to save. Sentimental items. Practical pieces for putting together a new life. At this stage I know I'm selecting more than I need, probably more than I can carry; but the amount that I'm leaving behind is far greater. Deciding what to keep & where comes after the survey. All the rest will be turned over to "scavengers."
#moverR

Day 2, midday break, Monday.
Today is harder than yesterday. I'm uncovering far more "sentimental" stuff. The challenge: these saved memories are a physical drag that impair rather than contribute anything practically to my future AND I feel integrated looking at them. There's a strong general condemnation, I feel, of holding onto sentimental items. Yet I know that they are powerful. But hey, I'm an artist. Even wistful, sad memories are valuable to me. I'm proceeding as before, retaining less+

+than I'm tossing, while knowing that I am keeping "too much." The next phase will be the actual moving & storing phase when more might be cut. This phase is the pre-screen before turning the cleared items over to others who will take everything away.
File under: how to move out of a home of 12 years & a home town of 45 years, with family roots here (& artifacts) from 150 years. I'm finally leaving, in search of a culture that suits my values.
#moverR
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
fárrenprošeakta, giđđalohpoviervvuheapmi
1. beaivi:
Čorgen váldo-oađđenlanja skábe
& mátkiid sisdoalu ovtta seaidnis.
Mun dárkkistan buot iežan gávnniid. Váldit eret unna oasáža maid seastit. Muitogávnnit. Geavatlaš ávdnasat ođđa eallima huksemii. Dán muttus dieđán ahte válljen eanet go dárbbashan, várra eanet go nagodan gávvut; muhto dat maid guđán maŋis lea ollu eanet. Maŋŋel dárkkisteami mearridan maid seastit & gosa. Buot eará guđđojuvvo "gávdnuide".
#moverR

2. beaivi, gaska-beaivve boddu, mánnodat.
Ovddeš beaivi lei álkibut go dát ođđasit. Gávdnan ollu eanet "muitogávnniid". Hástalus: dát seastojuvvon muittožiid dárffat leat rumašlaš lossadusat mat eambbo gozihit go addit eallimii maidege geavatlaččat JA mun dovddan iežan ollisin go geahčan daid. Doivvon ahte olbmot dábálaččat garrasit hálbvašit muitogávnniid seastima. Alitgo dieđán ahte dain lea fápmu. Muhto hei, mun lean dáiddár. Maiddái dološ, váibmolaš muittut leat munnje divrasat. Joatkkán nugo ovdalge, seasttán unnit+

+go maid bálkestat, vaikko dieđán ahte seasttán "liihtto". Boahtte muddu lea ieš dat fárren- & rádjanmuddu, goas várra eambbo sáhttá váldot eret. Dát muddu lea ovda-gursun ovdalgo čorgejuvvon gávnnit sirdojuvvojit earáide geat vikit buot eret.
Fiila dán vuollai: mo fárret eret dakkár ruovttus gos lea orron 12 jagi & gávpogis gos lea orron 45 jagi, gos bearraša ruohttasat (ja dološ dávvirat) leat leamaš 150 jagi. Lean dál viimmat vulgimin, ohcamin kultuvrra mii heive iežan árvvuide.
#moverR

Dán muttus dieđán ahte válljen eanet go dárbbahan, várra eanet go nagodan guoddit; muhto dat maid guđán maŋis lea ollu eanet. Easka geahčadeami maŋŋel mearridan maid seasttán & gosa. Buot eará guđđojuvvo "bázahusgáidduseddjiide".
#moverR

2. beaivi, gaskabeaivveboddu, mánnodat.
Odne lea lossat eambbo go ikte. Gávnnan ollu eanet "muitogávnniid". Hástalus: dát seastojuvvon muittut leat nugo lossa noađđi mii eambbo goziha go veahkeha mu eallimis geavatlaččat JA dovddan iežan olles olmmožin go geahčan daid. Dovddan ahte olbmot dábálaččat garrasit dálostit dakkár muitogávnniid seastima. Alitgo dieđán ahte dain lea fápmu. Muhto hei, mun lean dáiddár. Maiddái dološ, váibmolaš ja váidalahtti muittut leat munnje divrasat. Joatkkán nugo ovdalge, seasttán unnit+

+go maid bálkestan, vaikko dieđán ahte seasttán "liiggebeare". Boahtte muddu lea ieš dat fárren- & rádjanmuddu, goas várra eambbo sáhttá eret váldot. Dát muddu lea ovda-gursun ovdalgo sirddán čorgejuvvon gávnniid earáide geat vikit buot eret.
Vuorkká dán vuollai: mo fárret eret ruovttus gos lea orron 12 jagi & ruovttugielddas gos lea orron 45 jagi, gos bearraša ruohttasat (ja dološ dávvirat) leat leamaš 150 jagi. Lean dál viimmat vulgimin, ohcamin kultuvrra mii heive iežan árvvuide.
#moverR

Son humai badjel guokte minuhta almmá oktage dievdu livččii gaskkalduhttán su, ja dajai dakkár áššiid go, ahte don fertet leat hoavda, muhto don it oaččo leat bahá.
Don fertet leat láigi, muhto it menddo láigi.
Don it oaččo goassige boarisnuvvat, it goassige leat hávvadahtti, itge goassige čájehuvvat.
Oi, ándagassii.
Min dievdolágan direktevra muitala munnje, ahte mun ferten geargat dás, vai beasan ovdanbuktit America suinna.
Oaidnit su bálkkašeami.
Lea dat nissonolmmoš, geasa son doallat dan sága.
Siihtet buresboahtin Margot Robbie.

source_doc:
@hacks4pancakes What do you think is actually going on?
Confused? Anxious? Executive Dysfunction? Unrealistic? Bone idle?
(I'm NOT confusing ND with idleness in my internal ontology here, but I wonder if either might be contributing...)

@hacks4pancakes @DamonHD Foe emigration at least, I can imagine myself saying “oh I’m so jealous, I would LOVE to do that” when the truth is there are internal or external reasons why I wouldn’t/couldn’t and someone offering to help me with the logistics wouldn’t change that so I politely decline.

@darryl @hacks4pancakes The grown-up way to politely decline would be "I got ahead of myself: I cannot do that for other reasons much as I'd like to", not brushing it off as a diary issue!
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
@hacks4pancakes Maid don jáhkát duođas dáhpáhuvvat?
Skoalvvaduvvon? Biestalan? Jođihanhehttehus? Realisttahemet? Jalla laiki?
(In leat seahkaneddjiid dás iežan siskkáldas jurddašeamis eamiálbmotvuođa ja laikivuođa, muhto smiehtan ahte sáhttágo goabbáge váikkuhit...)

@hacks4pancakes @DamonHD Unnimustá fárrema hárrái sáhtán govahallat iežan loahpahit: ”O, lean nu gadehaš, háliidivččen NUU dán dahkat”, vaikko duohtavuohta lea ahte gávdnojit siskkáldas dahje olgguldas sivat manne in sáhte dahje háliidivčče, ja dat ahte oktage veahkehivččii mu lágidemiiguin ii rievdadivčče dan, nu ahte gielddán viisásit.

@darryl @hacks4pancakes Rávesolbmo vuohki gieldit viisásit livččii dadjat: ”Lean beare háskes, in sáhte dahkat dán eará sivaid geažil vaikko háliidivččen”, iige beare hilgut dan kaleandarkuorran!

Eahpesihkarvuohta? Biestaleapmi? Jođihanhehttehus? Realisttaheapme? Jalla laiki?
(In leat dás seahkaneddjiid neurodivergensa ja laikivuođa iežan jurddašeamis, muhto smiehtan ahte sáhttágo goabbáge váikkuhit...)

@hacks4pancakes @DamonHD Unnimustá eará riikii fárrema hárrái sáhtán govahallat iežan loahpahit: ”O, lean nu gadehaš, háliidivččen NUU dán dahkat”, vaikko duohtavuohta lea ahte gávdnojit siskkáldas dahje olgguldas sivat manne in háliidivčče/sáhtáše, ja dat ahte oktage veahkehivččii mu lágidemiiguin ii rievdadivčče dan, nu ahte gielddán siviila vuogi mielde.

@darryl @hacks4pancakes Rávesolbmo vuohki gieldit siviila vuogi mielde livččii dadjat: ”Lean hálestuvvan ovdalgihtii: in sáhte dahkat dán eará sivaid geažil vaikko háliidivččen”, iige beare hilgut dan nugo dat livččii kaleandarčuolbma!

source_doc:
I wish alcohol wasn't such a normal gift in society 😅 My employer sent me a bottle of eggnog but the only alcohol we like is in alcohol markers

I guess "not liking it" is kinda poor wording, I just think it's weird how casual we are with this stuff and we somehow just assume that all adults love this shit when there's people who shouldn't be touching it ever, and frankly I think the world would be better off if I poured it into the toilet than even gifting it to someone

@doombloomart
I have had US Big Corp required sensitivity training that reminds people not everyone wants alcohol and it's inappropriate to ask why. Seems like that should be more common

@elithebearded Yeah, that's mostly what I wish for too. I get that with sending random shit to a whole corporation will never be inoffensive to everyone, but 1. don't send random shit to a whole corporation maybe and 2. alcohol just rubs me wrong in the way that many who shouldn't touch it may touch it when it randomly shows up at the doorstep. 😅

Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Sáhtášedje go dakkár skeaŋkat go alkohola unnut leat dábálaččat servodagas 😅 Mu bargoaddi sáddii munnje bollu mánnodávvalgáffea (eggnog), muhto áidna alkohola mas mii liikojit, lea alkoholatusšain

Dán "eat liiko" lea kánske veahá boastut daddjojuvvon, mu mielas lea dušše dakkár outo ášši man dábálaš dat lea ja mii dušše jáhkkit ahte buot rávvásat liikojit dán fasttes málvii. Leat olbmot geat eai galgá oppa guoskatge dása, ja riekta daddjojuvvon, mu mielas máilbmi livččii buoret báiki jos luittošin dán dušše hivsegii baicca ko skeajehivččen dán geasage

@doombloomart
Mus lea leamaš dakkár obligáhtoralaš čuovvun hárjehallan US Big Corp:as, mii muittuha olbmuide ahte eai buohkat hálit alkohola ja ahte lea eahpeheivvolaš jearrat manne. Orru leamen ahte dát galggašii leat dábálaččat

@elithebearded Juo, dat lea eanas maid munge sávan. Mun ádden ahte dakkár sáddehusaid sádden olles fitnodahkii ii goasge sáhte leat dakkár mii ii hávvát geandage, muhto 1. ale sádde dakkár dušše duššat olles fitnodahkii ja 2. alkohola dušše hárre mu go nu dakkárat geat eai galggašii dása guoskat, sáhttet dan dahkat go dat fáhkka ihtá uvsse duohkái. 😅
hypo_1:
Sáren alkohola ii livčče ná dábálaš skeaŋka servodagas 😅 Bargoaddi sáddii munnje lulbmoniehttemálve-boanddu (eggnog), muhto áidna alkohola mas mii liikojit, lea alkoholatusšain

Dán "eat liiko" lea kánske veahá jalla daddjojuvvon, mu mielas lea dušše dakkár outo ášši man dábálažžan mii dán dálkkodit ja mii dušše jáhkkit ahte buot rávvásat liikojit dán pasfeide, go leat olbmot geat eai galggašii oppa guoskatge dása. Ja duođaid, mu mielas máilbmi livččii buoret báiki jos luittošin dán dušše hivsegii baicca go skeajehivččen dán geasage

@doombloomart
Mus lea leamaš dakkár US Big Corp neavrresvuođahárjehallan, mii muittuha olbmuide ahte eai buohkat hálit alkohola ja ahte ii heive jearrat manne. Orru leamen ahte dát galggašii leat dábálaččat

@elithebearded Juo, dat lea eanas maid munge sávan. Mun ádden ahte dakkár duššasáddemat olles fitnodahkii eai goasge sáhte leat dakkárat mat eai hávvádahtá geandage, muhto 1. ale sádde dakkár duššasáddemiid olles fitnodahkii ja 2. alkohola dušše fasttoda mu mielas go nu dakkárat geat eai galggašii dása guoskat, sáhttet dan dahkat go dat fáhkka ihtá uvsse duohkái. 😅
hypo_2:
Sáren alkohola ii livčče ná dábálaš skeaŋka servodagas 😅 Bargoaddi sáddii munnje bohtala málveboanddu, muhto áidna alkohola mas mii liikojat lea alkoholatusšain

Ja duođaid, mu mielas máilbmi livččii buoret báiki jos luittošin dán hivsegii baicca go skeajehivččen dán geasage

source_doc:
@la Isn't rule #1 of machining that if you haven't faced it off, it's not square or flat?

@azonenberg yes, of course, but I *did* mean to face it off. I just had my tool zero set wrong.
Because I assumed that it was roughtly flat.
I'm making an extra part today. I have the stock. And if I don't it will bug me to no end. Plus, during test fitting the bearing I broke the glue holding the part on my plate, so I would have to indicate it back in anyways, which I don't wanna dooooo, so.....

@la I know the feeling.
Had a similar problem when working on my house where literally every datum you might think of using was wrong.
I had a contractor frame a door frame plumb and square. Had to rip out a couple of studs after he left because the door needed to be like a degree off level in order to be flush with the floor and not floating in the air on one side.
Another (partial height) wall has each stud carefully cut to length because no two are the same size... The floor isn't flat so to make the countertop level you need the wall to be a parallelogram rather than a rectangle
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
@la Almma fal reanddaid duddjomis njuolggadus #1 lea ahte jos it leat gurašan dan, de dat ii leat njuolgat iige duolbbas?

@azonenberg jua, dieđusge, muhto mu áigumuš *lei* gurašit dan. Mu reanddu nolla lei juste feaila biddjon.
Daningo jáhkken ahte dat lei sullii duolbbas.
Ráhkadan badjelmearálaš oasi odne. Mus lea ávnnas. Ja jos in ráhkat, de dat dasti miella háhkat munnje álo. Lassin, go geahččalin makkár gierdi lea, de doddjon liima mii doalai oasi mu binnás, nu ahte livččen ferten bidjat dan fas njuolga, maid in hálit bargat, nu ahte.....

@la Dovddan dan dovddu.
Mus lei sullasaš váttisvuohta go barggen mu dáluin, gos njuolga juohke áidna dilli maid sáhtii jurddašit geavahit lei feaila.
Mus lei fitnodat mii hápmii uksašárpma njuolga ja dássemetrilaccat. Fertejin gaikut moadde stoalppu maŋŋel go son vulggii, daningo uksa fertii leat sullii gráda eahpenjuolga vai dat livččii seamma dásis go muorraluovvi iige leat áimmus nubbájit bealde.
Nubbi (belohahkii) sádjo mii lea sárguduvvon nu ahte juohke stoalpu lea dárkilit čuollon iežas gukkodahkii daningo eai guokte leat seamma stuorrát... Luovvi ii leat duolbbas, nu ahte vai beavdi šaddá njuolga, de fertet sádjo leat parallellográmma iige rektáŋgel

Mus lei fitnodat mii hápmii uksašárpma njuolga ja dássemetrilaccat. Fertejin gaikut moadde stoalppu maŋŋel go son vulggii, daningo uksa fertii leat sullii gráda eahpenjuolga vai dat livččii seamma dásis go muorraluovvi iige leat áimmus nubbájit bealde.
Nubbi (belohahkii gárvves) sádjo mii lea sárguduvvon nu ahte juohke stoalpu lea dárkilit čuollon iežas gukkodahkii daningo eai guokte leat seamma stuorrát... Luovvi ii leat duolbbas, nu ahte vai beavdi šaddá njuolga, de fertet sádjo leat parallellográmma iige rektáŋgel
hypo_7:
@la Almma fal reanddaid duddjomis njuolggadus #1 lea, ahte jos it leat gurašan dan, de dat ii leat njuolgat iige duolbbas?

@azonenberg jua, dieđusge, muhto mu áigumuš *lei* gurašit dan. Mu reanddu nolla lei juste feaila biddjon.
Daningo jáhkken ahte dat lei sullii duolbbas.
Ráhkadan badjelmearálaš oasi odne. Mus lea ávnnas. Ja jos in ráhkat, de dat boahtá dasti giddet mu millii álo. Lassin, go geahččalin makkár gierdi lea, de doddjon liima mii doalai oasi mu binnás, nu ahte livččen ferten bidjat dan fas njuolga, maid in hálit bargat, nu ahte.....

source_doc:
My brother in He-Who-Shall-Not-Be-Named, what you are describing is a consequence of _misogyny_, not "misandry."

I desperately need men to realize that just because it's directed at them and an actual serious problem doesn't mean it is "misandry."
Just saw someone describe being discriminated against _for being considered effeminate_ as "misandry" and I just.

It's not that you aren't experiencing something real. You very well may be. But it isn't "misandry." Because "misandry" doesn't exist.
Yes, this applies to trans men as well. "Transmisandry" _also_ isn't a thing and just contributes to anti-feminist narratives. It treats itself as a mirror of transmisogyny, but transmisogyny is _intersective_: we experience transphobia _and_ we experience misogyny _and_ we experience this unholy thing that happens when the two interact. It's not a subset, even an exclusive subset, it's an intersection.
But the following all are absolutely problems that can be talked about!
* Transphobia/Transmisia
* Antitransmasculity
* Transemasculation
* Misogyny
It sucks to be discriminated against, but the language that we use is so, so important here.
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Mu viellja sutnje-geas-ii-galgga-namahit, dat maid don deskribet lea _misogynia_ (nissonvašši) konsekuensa, ii ge "misandria" (almmái- dahje dievdo-vašši).

Mun háliidivččen nu fasttit ahte dievdoolbmot áddešit, ahte dušše dan dihte go juoga čuohcá sidjiide ja lea duohta váttisvuohta, de dat ii daddjo "misandrian."
Giddejin aiddo fuomášumi dasa go oktage deskribii vealaheami _danin go su giddejedje nissonlágážin_ "misandrian" ja mun dušše.

Ii dasto nu ahte don it vásiše juoidá mii lea duohta. Sáhtát áinnas vásihit. Muhto dat ii leat "misandria." Danne go "misandria" ii gávdno.
Juo, dát guoská maid transdievduide. "Transmisandria" _ii maid ge_ leat makkárge ášši ja dušše doarju antifeministtalaš muitalusaid. Dat gieđahallá iežas dego transmisogynia speadjallan, muhto transmisogynia lea _intersekšunála_: mii vásihit transphobia _ja_ mii vásihit misogynia _ja_ mii vásihit dán bastilis ášši mii gávdná sadji go dát guokte deaivvadit. Dat ii leat dušše oasáš, vaikko vel sierra oasášge, dat lea intersekšuvdna.
Muhto čuovvovaš áššit leat dattetge duohta váttisvuođat maiguin sáhttá hupmat!
* Transphobia/Transmisia
* Antitransmaskulinitehta
* Transemasgulašuvdna
* Misogynia
Lea fasti go vealahuvvo, muhto giela mii geavahit lea nu, nu dehalas dás.

Dat gieđahallá iežas dego transmisogynia speadjallan, muhto transmisogynia lea _intersekšunála_: mii vásihit transfobiija _ja_ mii vásihit misogynia _ja_ mii vásihit dán fasttes ášši mii gávdná saji go dát guokte deaivvadit. Dat ii leat oasáš, vaikko vel sierra oasášge, dat lea intersekšuvdna.
Muhto čuovvovaš áššit leat dattetge duohta váttisvuođat maiguin sáhttá hupmat!

instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is automatically transcribed from spoken language and can contain errors. Maintain the flow and colloquial style of the speaker in the translation. Do not include non-linguistic sounds (e.g. laughter, groans, hesitation sounds, etc.), but do include interjections. If a word is interrupted, either guess the full word if possible or otherwise omit it. Keep foreign words as they are when translating. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Output the text such that each sentence is on a separate line. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Dán smávva lanja dás ferte de dál dásahit.
Mun ferten olu áššiid bálkestit eret.
Dása leat báhcán olu áššit dán dálon ovddeš eaiggádiin, ja mun in leat goassige riekta geahčadan dán čađa.
Sii mii livččii buoret áigi go dál go máilbmi geahččá?
Oalle moivi dán siste?
Cokka čuovgga.
Don oainnát ahte doppe leat muhtin hildut mas leat juoga mii.
Juo, mu mielas lea leamaš áigi dásahit dán juo guhká ja dál lea áigi.
Dás lea de makkárge skápma.
Sáhtán veddot mas mii beare ahte dieđán juste mii dát lea.
hypo_1:
Dán smávva lanja dás ferte de dál čorget.
Mun ferten bálkestit eret oalle olu dálvviid.
Dása leat báhcán olu dávvirat dán viesu ovddeš eaiggádiin, ja mun in leat goassige riekta mannan dán čađa.
Mii livččii buoret áigi go juste dál go máilbmi geahččá?
Oalle moivi dás siste?
Cokka čuovgga.
Don oainnát ahte doppe leat muhtin hildut main leat juvllat.
Juo, munnje lea leamaš áigumuš čorget dán juo guhká, ja dál lea áigi.
Dás lea de makkárge kássa.
Sáhtán veddot mielddisat mii beare ahte dieđán juste mii dát lea.
hypo_2:
Dán smávva lanja dás ferte dál čorget.
Mun ferten bálkestit eret oalle olu áššiid.
Dása leat báhcán olu dávvirat dán viesu ovddeš eaiggádiin, ja mun in leat goassige ektui mannan dán čađa.
Mii livččii buoret áigi go juste dál go máilbmi geahččá?
Oalle moivi dás siste, vai nu gohčoduvvon?
Cokka čuovgga.
Don oainnát ahte doppe leat muhtin hildut main leat moadde dávvira.
Juo, munnje lea leamaš áigumuš čorget dán juo guhká, ja dál lea áigi.
Dás lea dál makkárge kássa.
Sáhtán veddot mielddisat mii beare ahte dieđán juste mii dát lea.
hypo_3:
Dán unna lanjatža dás ferte dál čorget.
Mun ferten bálkestit eret oalle olu gálvguid.
Dán viesu ovddeš eaiggádiin leat báhcán olu dávvirat dohko, ja in leat goassige riekta mannan dán čađa.
Mii livččii buoret áigi go juste dál go máilbmi lea geahččamin?
Oalle moivi dás siste, vai mii?
Cokka čuovgga.
Oainnát ahte doppe leat moadde hildu main leat moadde gálvvu.

Orut dál.

It's hurt.
It's a piece of metal.
What are you talking about?
He needs help.
So we should help him.
Look, I don't have the supplies to salvage another robot.
I certainly don't need another one.
Look at it.
You think it's hurt?
I think it needs help.
It's a robot.
It's a chunk of scrap and wires.
I don't know if you're glitched out or something, but we really have to go.
You hear that, thunder?
I hope you can compute what that means.

Dalle lea bávččagan.
Dat lea metallabihttá.
Man birra don hálat?
Son dárbbaša veahki.
Nuba mii fertet veahkehit su.
Gula, mus eai leat veahkkeneavvut eará bova gádjumii.

Gulatgo don dan, álddagas?
Mun sávan don dádjut maid dat mearkkaša.
